title: uva chamae
intro: Uva chamae, also known as the Bearberry or Kinnikinnick, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to North America with small white or pink flowers and red berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its astringent and antiseptic properties, particularly for treating urinary tract infections and kidney stones. The medicinal properties of Uva chamae are attributed to its high arbutin content, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and reduce inflammation, resulting in improved health benefits such as reduced symptoms of UTIs, kidney stones, and even some cases of diabetes. Herbal preparations of Uva chamae include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to support urinary health and overall well-being.
